single point overall responsibility for execution & completion of the total job.
a) The Bidder should have successfully completed similar works* (as defined
below) during the last 10 years from the original bid due date of tender. The
value of the works completed (proof of completion to be submitted) should
be as follows:

i) The bidder’s average annual financial turnover during the last 3 years,
ending 31st March of the previous financial year, shall be Rs 596 Lakhs or
higher.

Criteria
sheet.

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Note: - The values mentioned are basic contract value without taxes and duties.
text:
Note: - The values mentioned are basic contract value without taxes and duties.
Definition of Similar Work :
Bidder shall have completed one New Fired Heater Job OR Revamp Package Job for a Heater in
Hydrocarbon service OR Steam Reformer furnace OR Steam Cracker furnace, including carrying
out residual engineering, procurement (of the pressure parts), fabrication, supply and erection in
any upstream and downstream industry involved in crude production and processing /
Petrochemical plants / Chemical process plants / Hydrocarbon gas processing/ Power plants in
India or abroad in the preceding past 10 years from the original bid due date of tender.
A job executed and completed by a bidder as a Sub-contractor shall be considered for the purpose
of meeting the experience criteria of BQC subject to submission of following documents in
support of meeting the “Bidder Qualification Criteria”:
i. Copy of work order along with SOR issued by main Contractor.
ii. Copies of Completion Certificate(s) from the main Contractor.

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC):
text:
iii. In addition, copies of Completion Certificate(s) from the end User/ Owner/ Project
Management Consultant (PMC) mentioning that the work has been executed by bidder as a sub-
contractor or a copy of approval by end User/Owner for engaging the bidder as Sub-contractor.
The Completion Certificate(s) shall have details like work order no., date, brief scope of work,
ordered & executed value of the job, completion date etc. as required for qualification of the
bidder in line with BQC.
In case of composite contracts, wherein similar work forms a part of the contract, the executed
value of the similar works only shall be considered for evaluation. Break-up of the executed
value to be submitted by the bidder with client certification.
All above clauses shall be read in conjunction.

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Clause 10 Financial Documents mandatory, a declaration from CFO/CEO will be required
text: 10 Financial Documents mandatory, a declaration from CFO/CEO will be required for self-certification of financial documents. i. Submission of authentic documents is the prime responsibility of the bidder. Wherever BPCL has concern or apprehension regarding the authenticity / correctness of any document, BPCL reserves the right of getting the documents cross verified from the document issuing authority. In case of non-submission of the required documents or ambiguity or incomplete documents, BPCL reserves the right to reject the bidder's tender without assigning any reason. ii. BPCL reserves the right to assess the capability and capacity of the bidder. iii. BPCL reserves the right to verify the successful completion of qualifying jobs & bidder shall arrange permissions for visiting these installations. iv. BPCL shall not be responsible for any expense incurred by bidders in connection with the preparation & delivery of their bids, site visit and other expenses incurred during bidding process and all such expenses shall be borne by the bidder. v. The bidder shall agree to BQC and shall submit required documents in support of the same, failing which the offer will be liable for rejection. Note:- During evaluation and comparison of bids, the purchaser may, at his discretion, ask the bidder for clarifications on the bid. The request for clarification shall be given asking the tenderer to respond by a specified date. If the tenderer does not comply or respond by the date, his tender will be liable to

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Clause 10 Financial Documents mandatory, a declaration from CFO/CEO will be required
text: the bidder for clarifications on the bid. The request for clarification shall be given asking the tenderer to respond by a specified date. If the tenderer does not comply or respond by the date, his tender will be liable to be rejected. No change in prices or substance of the bid shall be sought, offered or permitted . No post bid clarification at the initiative of the bidder shall be entertained. The shortfall information / documents shall be sought only in case of historical documents which pre-existed at the time of the tender opening and which have not undergone change since then. So far as the submission of documents is concerned with regard to qualification criteria, after

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC):

submission of the tender, only related shortfall documents shall be asked for and considered. For
example, if the bidder has submitted a work order without its completion / performance
certificate, the certificate can be asked for and considered. However, no new work order shall be
considered for BQC qualification.
Earnest Money Deposit (EMD) :-

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Clause 1 The interest-free Earnest Money Deposit (EMD) of Rs.10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs
text:
1. The interest-free Earnest Money Deposit (EMD) of Rs.10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs
Only) to be submitted by bidder. The EMD shall be generally arranged prior to the Bid
Submission Due Date & time as mentioned in the etender or corrigendum thereof.

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Clause 2 The tenderer must submit/ deposit earnest money, failing which the tender is liable to be
text:
2. The tenderer must submit/ deposit earnest money, failing which the tender is liable to be
rejected.

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Clause 3 Earnest Money Deposit (EMD) shall be valid for a period of 6 (Six) months from the due
text:
3. Earnest Money Deposit (EMD) shall be valid for a period of 6 (Six) months from the due
date of opening of Techno-commercial Bids and shall be submitted from any Indian Scheduled
Commercial Bank / Indian Branch of Foreign Bank.

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Clause 4 No interest shall be paid by the Owner on the earnest money deposit by the tenderer.
text: 4. No interest shall be paid by the Owner on the earnest money deposit by the tenderer.

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Clause 5 The permissible forms of EMD are:
text:
5. The permissible forms of EMD are:
Electronic Funds Transfer to BPCL's Bank Account as mentioned below.
NAME OF THE BANK - STANDARD CHARTERED BANK
NAME OF THE BRANCEH - M.G. ROAD
ADDRESS OF THE BANK - 90, M G ROAD, FORT, MUMBAI – 400 001
NEFT IFSC CODE - SCBL0036001
9 DIGIT MICR CODE - 400036002
TYPE OF A/C - 11 (CURRENT)
ACCOUNT NO. – 22205020115
For refund of EMD deposited through NEFT, vendor must have vendor code issued by BPCL.
In case it is not available, vendor need to submit requisite documents as communicated by BPCL.
Once vendor code is made available then only EMD shall be refunded.
a) Bank Guarantee executed by any Scheduled Bank approved by Reserve Bank of India
(as per Proforma provided in Annexure of GCC).
b) Insurance Surety Bond (as per Proforma provided in Annexure)
The following categories of bidders are however exempted from depositing EMD:-
a) Units registered with National Small Scale Industrial Corporation Limited (NSIC).
b) Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) registered with District Industries Centres (DIC) or
Khadi and Village Industries Commission or Khadi and Village Industries Board or Coir Board
or National Small Industries Corporation (NSIC) or Directorate of Handicraft and Handloom or
MSEs having Udyog Aadhaar Memorandum or any other body specified by Ministry of Micro,
Small and Medium Enterprises.

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC):
text: The above are subject to the fulfilling of under mentioned conditions:

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC):
text: i.Bidder shall submit Self attested copy of the certificate, valid upto validity of the offer, indicating that their registration includes the items/works under tender. ii.The registration certificate should remain valid during the period of the contract that may be entered into such successful bidder. Such tenderers should ensure validity of the Registration Certificate for the purpose. iii. Registration with DGS&D will not entitle a Tenderer to claim above exemption. iv.Such bidders must upload appropriate proof at location specified for the same in the etender, to show that they are eligible for the exemption from EMD (application for registration as NSIC / Udyam registration / MSE or for renewal will not be acceptable), failing which such bid will be treated as bid received without EMD and liable to be rejected. v. All MSEs who are having Udyog Aadhaar Memorandum (UAM) /EM I/ EM II shall not be given benefits available under Public Procurement Policy for MSEs order 2012. They are required to file fresh registration in the new Udyam Registration Portal (https://udyamregistration.gov.in) and submit the same in tender for getting EMD as well as MSE purchase preference. All enterprises registered with MoMSME and whom registration have been re-classified in accordance with notification no S.O.2119 (E) dated 26/06/2020, they are required to submit Udyam Registration certificate. vi.Bidders must have to submit copy of MSE registration certificate along with CA certificate (Exempted in case of NSIC submitted). In case CA certificate in not

text: notification no S.O.2119 (E) dated 26/06/2020, they are required to submit Udyam Registration certificate. vi.Bidders must have to submit copy of MSE registration certificate along with CA certificate (Exempted in case of NSIC submitted). In case CA certificate in not submitted, bid of the bidder shall be rejected at EMD stage.

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Clause 6 In case start up is a MSE, Purchase preference shall be extended as per MSE policy.
text:
6. In case start up is a MSE, Purchase preference shall be extended as per MSE policy.
However, for non MSE startups, only EMD exemption shall be extended.

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Clause 7 The Bank Guarantee in lieu of EMD shall be furnished on non-judicial stamp paper of
text:
7. The Bank Guarantee in lieu of EMD shall be furnished on non-judicial stamp paper of
appropriate value and in the prescribed Performa given in the Tender Document.

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Clause 8 Bank Guarantee (BG) shall be executed by any Scheduled Bank approved by Reserve
text:
8. Bank Guarantee (BG) shall be executed by any Scheduled Bank approved by Reserve
Bank of India as per the proforma. The BG shall remain valid for a period of six months from
the due date of opening the tender.

Copy the BG/ Insurance Surety Bond as the case may be, along with proof of dispatch to be

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Clause 10 EMD of other unsuccessful bidders shall be released after issuance of FOA/LOA/contract
text:
10. EMD of other unsuccessful bidders shall be released after issuance of FOA/LOA/contract
Order against this tender. However, in case of successful bidder the EMD shall be converted to
Security Deposit and shall be released on receipt of performance bank guarantee if applicable for
contract.

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Clause 11 All the bids of a bidder who has submitted multiple bids, as per clause 13.12 of GCC,
text:
11. All the bids of a bidder who has submitted multiple bids, as per clause 13.12 of GCC,
shall be rejected and Bid security/EMD of all such bids shall be forefeited.

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Clause 12 A bidder's EMD will be forfeited if the bidder withdraws or amends its/his tender or
text:
12. A bidder's EMD will be forfeited if the bidder withdraws or amends its/his tender or
impairs or derogates from the tender in any respect within the period of validity of the tender or
if the successful bidder fails to furnish the required performance security within the specified
period in applicable cases.

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Clause 13 If contractor commits any breach of the terms and conditions of tender / contract, action
text:
13. If contractor commits any breach of the terms and conditions of tender / contract, action
shall be taken to realize the EMD in the form of BG from the Bank.

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Clause 14 The Earnest Money deposited by successful tenderer shall be forfeited if the successful
text:
14. The Earnest Money deposited by successful tenderer shall be forfeited if the successful
tenderer fails to honour the offer terms prior to ordering and Contractual terms after issuance of
FOA/LOA/contract Order.

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Clause 15 Offers received without scan copy of EMD (BG/Valid NSIC Certificate/ Insurance
text:
15. Offers received without scan copy of EMD (BG/Valid NSIC Certificate/ Insurance
Surety Bond) in the e-tender and physically not received within 7 days after Bid Submission Due
Date (as indicated in the etender or corrigendum thereof) are liable to be rejected.

breadcrumb: BID QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC) > 1.0 BID-QUALIFICATION CRITERIA (BQC): > Note: - Bidder is strictly advised not to make any online transaction to make payment against
text:
Note: - Bidder is strictly advised not to make any online transaction to make payment against
EMD vide NEFT/IMPS/RTGS in BPCL account mentioned above. This online payment shall
not be considered for EMD evaluation purposes. The details mentioned above are particularly
for Bank Guarantee purposes only.
